title: tax — Eloquent observers
description: Four observers enforce the module's write-time invariants: rate creation with effective-range validation + overlap detection, jurisdiction with ISO 3166 validation, exemption with certificate URL + verification workflow, and calculation with the immutable snapshot pattern.

handles: creating → populate ULID prefix `txr_`
creating → refuse when tenant does NOT hold `tax_capture` entitlement (returns `TAX_FEATURE_NOT_ENTITLED`)
creating → validate percentage in range [0.0000, 1.0000] (returns `TAX_RATE_INVALID_PERCENTAGE` — 100%+ tax rates almost always indicate misconfiguration)
creating → validate effective_from < effective_to when both set (returns `TAX_RATE_INVALID_EFFECTIVE_RANGE`)

creating → refuse overlapping (tenant_id, tax_jurisdiction_id, rate_type, applies_to, effective_range) via range-overlap query (returns `TAX_RATE_OVERLAPPING_RANGE` — two concurrent rates for the same tuple would make resolution ambiguous)
creating → default source='manual' when not provided (source='provider_synced' is set explicitly by SyncTaxRatesFromProviderJob)
creating → fire TaxRateCreated after commit
updating (percentage / effective_from / effective_to changed) → validate the new value against the same rules as create

updating → refuse when any tax_calculations row has referenced this rate via tax_rate_snapshot.rate_id (returns `TAX_CALCULATION_IMMUTABLE` — historical calculations must resolve to the exact rate that applied at issuance; rate mutation would corrupt the audit trail; instead, create a new rate with effective_from = today and set effective_to on the old rate)
updating → fire TaxRateUpdated
deleting (soft-delete) → refuse when any active tax_calculations references this rate (returns `TAX_CALCULATION_IMMUTABLE` — same rationale)
deleting (hard-delete) → refuse always (rates retain indefinitely for audit — the module has no soft-delete on tax_rates; effective_to <= today handles the 'expired' state without removing the row)
creating → populate ULID prefix `txj_`
creating → validate country_code against ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 catalog via league/iso3166 (returns `TAX_JURISDICTION_INVALID_COUNTRY`)
creating → validate state_code (when set) against ISO 3166-2 subdivisions for the country_code (returns `TAX_JURISDICTION_INVALID_STATE`)
creating → validate postal_code_pattern is a valid PCRE regex when set (returns `TAX_JURISDICTION_INVALID_POSTAL_PATTERN`)
creating → refuse when tenant_id IS NOT NULL AND tenant does NOT hold `tax_jurisdiction_custom` entitlement (returns `TAX_JURISDICTION_CUSTOM_NOT_ENTITLED` — Enterprise-only)
creating → refuse duplicate composite (tenant_id, country_code, state_code, city, postal_code_pattern, tax_type) partial WHERE deleted_at IS NULL (returns `TAX_JURISDICTION_DUPLICATE`)
creating → refuse when tenant_id IS NULL AND actor does NOT hold `super_admin` role (returns `TAX_INSUFFICIENT_ROLE` — only platform-ops can create platform-reference jurisdictions)
creating → fire TaxJurisdictionCreated
updating (country_code / state_code / postal_code_pattern changed) → re-validate against ISO catalogs; refuse when invalid
updating → fire TaxJurisdictionUpdated
updating (effective_to set to a past date) → fire internal event TaxJurisdictionDeactivated → CascadeExemptionExpiryOnJurisdictionDeactivated hook triggers exemption expiry
deleting (soft-delete) → refuse when any tax_rates references this jurisdiction (RESTRICT FK enforces this at DB level; observer surfaces a clear error message)
creating → populate ULID prefix `txe_`
creating → refuse when tenant does NOT hold `tax_exemption_management` entitlement (returns `TAX_EXEMPTION_MANAGEMENT_NOT_ENTITLED`)
creating → validate valid_from < valid_until when both set (returns `TAX_EXEMPTION_INVALID_VALIDITY_RANGE`)

creating → validate certificate_document_url matches ^https://[tenant-bucket-prefix].s3.*.amazonaws.com/... (returns `TAX_EXEMPTION_INVALID_CERTIFICATE_URL` — must be an HTTPS S3 signed URL under the tenant-scoped bucket prefix; enforces cross-tenant isolation on certificate storage)

creating → validate cross-model tenant integrity: the referenced customer (User / Athlete / Organization) must belong to the same tenant_id as the exemption (defense-in-depth against cross-tenant FK smuggling)
creating → validate issuing_jurisdiction_id references a jurisdiction with tenant_id IS NULL (platform reference) OR the same tenant_id (returns `TAX_JURISDICTION_NOT_FOUND` when mismatch)

creating → refuse duplicate composite (tenant_id, customer_type, customer_id, exemption_type, issuing_jurisdiction_id) partial WHERE deleted_at IS NULL AND verification_status = 'verified' (returns `TAX_EXEMPTION_DUPLICATE` — a customer cannot have TWO verified exemptions of the same type for the same jurisdiction)
creating → encrypt certificate_document_url at rest
creating → set verification_status='pending' + verified_at=NULL + verified_by_user_id=NULL
creating → fire TaxExemptionCreated
creating → dispatch VerifyTaxExemptionJob when the issuing_jurisdiction has a registered #[AsExemptionVerifier] verifier

updating (verification_status changed) → enforce state machine: pending → verified / rejected; verified → expired (automatic only, via ExpireExemptionsJob). No transitions FROM verified back to pending. No un-reject. No un-expire. (returns `TAX_EXEMPTION_INVALID_STATUS_TRANSITION`)
updating (verification_status → verified) → require verified_by_user_id present (unless the transition is triggered by an automatic registry check) + set verified_at = now()
updating (verification_status → verified) → fire TaxExemptionVerified
updating (verification_status → rejected) → require rejection reason on metadata + fire TaxExemptionRejected
updating (verification_status → expired) → refuse manual — only ExpireExemptionsJob can transition to 'expired'
updating (certificate_document_url changed) → re-validate URL + re-encrypt + require re-verification (transition back to 'pending')
deleting (soft-delete) → refuse when any tax_calculations has referenced this exemption via exemption_applied jsonb (returns `TAX_CALCULATION_IMMUTABLE`)
deleting (hard-delete) → allow only when soft-deleted > 7 years ago (regulator audit retention)
creating → populate ULID prefix `txc_`
creating → refuse when tenant does NOT hold `tax_capture` entitlement (returns `TAX_FEATURE_NOT_ENTITLED`)
creating → require source_amount_cents AND source_currency both present (returns `TAX_CALCULATION_MISSING_AMOUNT_OR_CURRENCY`)
creating → validate source_currency is a valid ISO 4217 3-letter uppercase code (returns `TAX_CALCULATION_INVALID_CURRENCY`)

creating → refuse when source_currency != tenant.default_region.currency AND tenant does NOT hold `tax_multi_currency` entitlement (returns `TAX_CALCULATION_MULTI_CURRENCY_NOT_ENTITLED` — Medium+ only)

creating → refuse when tenant has a mandated provider config for the jurisdiction AND the calculation is being computed via NativeCalculator (returns `TAX_CALCULATION_PROVIDER_MANDATED` — fail-safe against under-charging on complex nexus jurisdictions)
creating → freeze tax_rate_snapshot jsonb (rate_id + percentage + jurisdiction + effective_at date) into the row
creating → freeze exemption_applied jsonb (certificate_number + exemption_type + issuing_jurisdiction + valid_from + valid_until) when an exemption was applied
creating → set calculated_at = now() (immutable timestamp — regulator audit)
creating → fire TaxCalculationCompleted
updating → REFUSE ALL — tax_calculations rows are IMMUTABLE after commit (returns `TAX_CALCULATION_IMMUTABLE`)

deleting → REFUSE ALL — tax_calculations retain 7 years (10 for Enterprise) via PurgeExpiredCalculationsJob migrating to tax_calculations_archive; no soft-delete + no hard-delete inside the module. The FK CASCADE on tenant_id cascades on TenantErased AFTER MigrateCalculationsToArchiveOnTenantErased runs (priority 20 vs the cascade's priority 50).
